title: Axis II
artist_persona: A contemporary artist working in large-format mixed media
description: A large-format Bohemian Eclectic-inspired composition that anchors the room. Bold gestural marks in #ded2bf and #997a4d dance across a subdued field, creating a visual anchor that draws the eye from the natural standing viewpoint. The scale commands attention while the palette remains in conversation with the room's balanced, neutral tones.
artwork_rationale: This piece responds to the room's Bohemian Eclectic architecture (high confidence) with its refined composition. The intimately scaled ceiling (3.4m) and bright crisp lighting conditions informed the scale and contrast. At 3.8m viewing distance, the large statement format fills the optimal field of view.
color_rationale: The palette draws from the room's dominant #ded2bf and accent #997a4d tones, ensuring mixed harmony with the existing neutral interior. The medium vibrancy level matches the room's subdued character.
image_prompt: A high-end digital artwork titled 'Axis II', large statement format. A large-format Bohemian Eclectic-inspired composition that anchors the room. Bold gestural marks in #ded2bf and #997a4d dance across a subdued field, creating a visual anchor that draws the eye from the natural standing viewpoint. The scale commands attention while the palette remains in conversation with the room's balanced, neutral tones. The piece is museum-quality, suitable for a luxury interior. Color palette centered on #ded2bf with #997a4d accents. Style: bold contemplative. The artwork should feel like a commissioned gallery piece — not generic, not templated, but intentionally made for this specific room with Bohemian Eclectic architecture.

recommended_frame.description: A deep tray frame in satin brushed brass that floats the artwork away from the wall, creating a subtle shadow gap that enhances the piece's sculptural presence.

recommended_lighting.description: A single adjustable gallery light with a warm 2700K LED, centered above the artwork. The 40° beam angle illuminates the entire piece without glare.

recommended_placement: Primary wall (top right quadrant), centered at eye level (200×150cm)
